name: Lee Blair
abstract: Lee L. Blair (October 10, 1903, Savannah, Georgia – October 15, 1966, New York City) was an American jazz banjoist and guitarist.

works.t: What Is This Thing Called Swing?

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: we sent him and got Lee when Red Allen came in the band... We were on two trumpets, you see... He stayed with us about three or four, about a month.

about_quotes.speaker: Danny Barker
about_quotes.evidence: He want to get Lee Blair, who had been with Jelly Roll, or Jeff Williams and different hell of a guitars players who had changed from guitar to banjo.
about_quotes.doc_title: Danny Barker — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: Lee Blair on banjo... and you; and those are 'Oil Well,' two takes of 'Load of Coal,' and 'Crazy Chords,' and 'Primrose Stomp.'
